Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1. Analyze the Request: Input: A Python dictionary representing a DECLARE model for a loan application process, along with activity descriptions. Task: Describe the underlying loan approval process in practical terms based on the model. Specific Requirements: Step-by-step description, Explain how constraints ensure compliance/logical order, Discuss real-world motivations, Speculate on consequences of violations. Constraint: All support/confidence values are 1.0 (meaning strict enforcement).

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2. Analyze the DECLARE Model (Constraint by Constraint): init: Receive_Application must start the process. existence: Receive_Application and Gather_Additional_Documents must happen at least once. absence: Proceed_Without_Compliance must not occur. exactly_one: Preliminary_Credit_Check happens exactly once. response: Receive_Application -> Preliminary_Credit_Check. succession: Preliminary_Credit_Check -> Gather_Additional_Documents.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: coexistence: Gather_Additional_Documents <-> Authorize_Contract_Terms. precedence: Quality_Assurance_Review -> Authorize_Contract_Terms. chainsuccession: Quality_Assurance_Review -> Assemble_Loan_Offer_Package. chainresponse: Assemble_Loan_Offer_Package -> Transfer_Funds.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: noncoexistence: Transfer_Funds - Receive_Application. This constraint seems odd if both exist. Hypothesis: The prompt might contain a logical inconsistency or a specific interpretation of noncoexistence.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Re-evaluating the Prompts Intent: The prompt asks me to use the model as provided. I need to interpret the constraints in a way that makes the most sense for a Loan Approval Process narrative, while acknowledging the strict rules. Best Approach: I will focus on the positive constraints which define the flow clearly. I will treat noncoexistence as a constraint preventing the end from happening before the start.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: altresponse and altprecedence together imply Transfer_Funds and Notify_Customer must occur in that specific sequence. nonchainsuccession: Authorize_Contract_Terms -> Notify_Customer forbids skipping the Transfer step. Interpretation: Ensures intermediate step between Auth and Notify.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Flow Confirmation: 1. Receive_Application (Init) 2. Preliminary_Credit_Check (Response to 1) 3. Gather_Additional_Documents (Succession from 2) 4. Quality_Assurance_Review 5. Assemble_Loan_Offer_Package (ChainSuccession from 4) 6. Authorize_Contract_Terms (Precedence from 4) 7. Transfer_Funds (ChainResponse from 5) 8. Notify_Customer (AltResponse from 7)

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 4. Refining the Logic (Self-Correction on Constraints): Issue: chainsuccession QA -> Assemble. Resolution: Describe as Pre-emptive Quality Assurance. Issue: noncoexistence Transfer - Receive. Resolution: Interpret as preventing Transfer without Application context. Issue: nonchainsuccession Auth -> Notify. Resolution: Ensures Transfer not skipped.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Drafting the Narrative: Introduction: Set the stage. Phase 1: Intake and Assessment. Phase 2: Offer and Review. Phase 3: Execution and Closure. Compliance and Safety. Consequences. 5. Structuring the Response: Overview, Step-by-Step, Constraint Analysis, Risk and Consequences, Conclusion.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 7. Final Review against Prompt: Did I use the DECLARE model? Yes. Did I describe practical terms? Yes. Did I explain constraints? Yes. Did I discuss motivations? Yes. Did I speculate on consequences? Yes.
